title: Artificial intelligence challenges copyright in content creation
summary: Meta removes user image generation, labels for AI-created music are proposed, and detection of synthetic texts increases.

title: Data centers under scrutiny for regulation, emissions, and financial risks
summary: Municipal moratoriums, increased carbon emissions, and credit rating downgrades mark the landscape for data centers.

title: Debate over AI's impact on employment intensifies
summary: OpenAI CEO says AI will create jobs, while other executives urge caution, and its use as an excuse for layoffs is criticized.

title: Space advances: reusable rockets, orbital mirrors, and microgravity studies
summary: China recovers a reusable rocket and reaches an asteroid, FCC approves space mirrors, and two female scientists will fly in 2027 to study menstruation in space.

title: Hardware news: processors, graphics cards, and peripherals
summary: New cases, power supplies, MiniLED monitors, and laptops with latest-gen GPUs highlight the hardware news.

title: Policies and regulations impact data centers, education, and tolls
summary: New federal rules condition aid to universities, data center agreements are investigated, and Catalonia reconsiders AP-7 tolls.

title: Finance and business strategies in technology and entertainment
summary: Disney evaluates a free ad-supported tier, OpenAI bets on families, and circular GPU financing draws criticism.

title: Scientific studies reveal new insights about sleep, aging, and nutrition
summary: Research highlights the role of napping in memory, the increase of genetic mutations in sperm of older men, and the use of invasive algae as a superfood.

title: Microsoft loses court case in Brazil and must restore Xbox account
summary: The court ordered Microsoft to return games to a hacked user and pay $400 in compensation.

title: Sony plans to eliminate physical discs for PlayStation games
summary: Executives sell their shares while the EU admits it cannot prevent Sony from abandoning the physical format.

title: Camera-less smart glasses bet on productivity
summary: Manufacturers like Even Realities avoid recording to focus on productivity and privacy tools.

title: Artificial intelligence revolutionizes university education
summary: Universities ban devices in law classes and an experiment reveals that students without AI get much lower grades.

title: The world of gaming: protests, modding, and digital rights
summary: Bethesda union protests layoffs, PlayStation consoles are modded, and Chinese courts uphold inheritance of gaming accounts.

title: Microsoft and Windows stir controversy among IT professionals
summary: Administrators criticize Windows 11 bloatware and a veteran reveals how Windows 95 detected installers by filename.

title: New software and artificial intelligence tools
summary: Claude Cowork automates office tasks, AI agents win at Slay the Spire, and GPT-5 solves a math problem, while new runtimes and terminal emulators emerge.

title: Infrastructure and energy: submarine cables and historical discoveries
summary: A cable supplying electricity to San Francisco goes out of service, and construction of a wind farm in Denmark uncovers a 17th-century ship.

title: Apple sues OpenAI and Meta faces lawsuits over addiction and patents
summary: Apple accuses OpenAI of secret theft, Meta appeals a verdict on social media addiction and patents an emotional monitoring system.

title: Human stories: slavery, entrepreneurship, and a lifesaving slushie machine
summary: A woman is freed after 55 years of slavery in Brazil, Steve Jobs' son seeks to cure cancer, and a slushie machine relieves heat in New York.

title: Cyberattacks and vulnerabilities compromise security
summary: An npm package infected with malware and the Balochistan police portal used for espionage put the security community on alert.

title: Historical and cultural curiosities: from talking seals to Vikings
summary: Anecdotes like a seal that spoke English, the origin of fried pizza, the Necronomicon, and a Viking textile factory show the richness of history.
